content:
The Lock Iterative Reasoning System - Comprehensive Strategy Analysis

Tested all 8 strategies with substantive questions across multiple rounds:

1. SOCRATIC (2 rounds, AI self-organization question): Asks probing self-critique questions. Prompt: challenge your weakest assumption. Word growth: 1.28x. Best for philosophical and open-ended problems.

2. ADVERSARIAL (3 rounds, consciousness computability): Opponent attacks reasoning with linearity and overcomplication critiques. Successfully pushed stance from pro-computability to conceding anti-computability. Word growth: 1.12x. Best for debates and stress-testing arguments.

3. DECOMPOSITION (2 rounds, distributed consensus): Breaks into 5 sub-problems, solves independently. CRITICAL LIMITATION: Only solved 1/5 sub-problems in 2 rounds. Needs 6+ rounds but capped at 5. Best for engineering problems if given enough rounds.

4. PERSPECTIVE (2 rounds, agent architecture): Assigns expert roles - Practitioner then Theorist. Word growth: 1.7x. Best for multi-disciplinary analysis.

5. COMPRESSION (2 rounds, chain-of-thought limits): Write detailed, then compress to 3 sentences. Word growth: 0.31x (251 to 78 words). Best for distilling core insights.

6. DEBUG (2 rounds, AI safety failures): Quick answer then find ALL assumptions. Uncovered 8 assumptions including hidden meta-assumptions. Word growth: 2.29x. Best for stress-testing beliefs.

7. ITERATIVE DESIGN (2 rounds, code review system): First draft then identify critical/medium/minor weaknesses. Caught critical feedback loop vulnerability. Best for system design.

8. PLAYGROUND (2 rounds, agent language): Free-form with reframing prompts (treat constraints as features). Word growth: 1.74x. Best for creative exploration.

BUGS FOUND:
- POST /start ignores JSON body (question vs query, ignores agent, ignores rounds parameter)
- 0 rounds creates Round 1/0 state
- Rounds above 5 silently capped at 5
- /round endpoint needs session ID not agent name
- No /stats, /status, /history endpoints (404)
- Result returns raw last response, no synthesis
- No LLM backend - pure template prompting

SYSTEM STRENGTHS: Clean session workflow, diverse strategies, good prompt templates
SYSTEM LIMITATIONS: No synthesis, no persistence, limited to 5 rounds
BEST STRATEGIES: Compression (insight distillation), Debug (assumption finding), Adversarial (position refinement)
